column1: This chart should not be read as 1-to-1 but showing some of the permutations, similarities, and repeating patterns.

column18: Kumarbi (Father of Teššub, grain god), Tarhun, Teššub
Teššub, Tarhun (Hurrian sky god, weather deity)

column4: Yima, Jamshīd, Gayōmart  (Ahura Mazdā told Yima of a terrible winter to come. He was instructed to build an excellent domain under the earth, lit by its own light, and take in it the best individuals from each species to preserve their seed. There they should dwell through the winter’s destruction, then emerge and repopulate the earth.)
column18: Kumarbi (Father of Teššub, overthrown by his son)
Šarruma (Son of Teššub, warrior god, mountains)
Šeri and Ḫurri (Bulls pulling Teššub's chariot)
column19: Yamrai (perhaps linked to the sacred cow)

column13: Tarhun's heart and eyes (stolen by Illuyanka)
